hero.body: Culture, history, wildlife, and landscape — curated across the continent by a team that calls it home. Drill Tours builds journeys with the precision of a private office and the warmth of a host who knows the ground.

mission.quote: To connect travelers with authentic African experiences — without compromise on service, safety, or comfort.

packages.heading: Eight ways to move through Africa.
packages.featured_callout: Featured this season: the Asafotufiami Festival, Ada →
packages.festival_body: The Ada people's annual homecoming on the Greater Accra coast — a re-enactment march to Luhuese, traditional drumming and mock battle displays, a grand durbar of chiefs, and beach festivities along the Ada coastline. The 89th edition begins the first week of August 2026.

items.q: Do I need a visa to travel to Ghana?
items.a: Most travelers do — Ghana doesn't offer walk-up visas on arrival for most nationalities. ECOWAS citizens travel visa-free for up to 90 days. Everyone else should plan to apply in advance. See our full visa guide for who needs what and how long it takes.

items.q: How does the reservation process actually work?
items.a: Submit the reservation form with your dates, destination, and group size — that's a request for availability and pricing, not a confirmed booking yet. A travel designer reviews it and replies, usually within one business day, with a firm itinerary and price. Nothing is charged until you confirm.

items.q: Can I get a quote before committing to anything?
items.a: Yes — that's exactly what the reservation form does. There's no obligation at the inquiry stage, and you're welcome to ask questions over WhatsApp before submitting anything formal.

items.q: Do you arrange travel outside Ghana?
items.a: Yes. Ghana is our home ground and where most of our on-the-ground experience is concentrated, but we arrange tours across West, East, Southern, North, and Central Africa.

items.q: What's included in a tour package price?
items.a: It depends on the package and is confirmed with your specific quote — typically transport, guiding, and entry fees are included, while flights and personal spending usually aren't.

items.q: Do you handle group, corporate, or institutional travel?
items.a: Yes — conferences, government delegations, NGO logistics, executive travel, and school or institutional excursions are a regular part of what we do.

items.q: What if I need to change or cancel my trip?
items.a: Cancellation terms vary by package, season, and any third-party suppliers involved. We'll confirm the specific terms that apply once your booking is underway.

items.q: How quickly will someone get back to me?
items.a: Within one business day for reservation and contact form submissions. For anything urgent, WhatsApp us directly — that gets picked up faster than email.

about.heading: Cultural heritage. Deeper understanding. Authentic Africa.

brafie.heading: The Brafie Project
brafie.body1: The Brafie Project is a Drill Tours programme that aims to facilitate the return of people of African descent to their ancestral homelands. More than a tour, it is a guided path back — to culture, to community, and to roots.
brafie.body2: The programme provides practical assistance with citizenship, investment opportunities, and establishing a business presence in Ghana — supporting the diaspora not just to visit, but to belong and to build.

return.heading: Return
return.body: Facilitating the physical and cultural return of the African diaspora to Ghana and across the continent.

citizenship.heading: Citizenship
citizenship.body: Guidance on Ghana's Right of Abode programme and the pathways available to people of African descent.

investment.heading: Investment
investment.body: Connecting returning diaspora with real investment and business opportunities in Ghana's growing economy.

community.heading: Community
community.body: Building lasting connections between the diaspora and local Ghanaian communities, institutions, and leaders.
